title: Wire bot.inventory.on('updateSlot') for real-time inventory
description:
## Problem
The current inventory tracking infers item counts from `BlockMinedEvent` events (`ApplyBlockMined → AddInventoryItem`), which is an inference, not ground truth. The actual Mineflayer hook `bot.inventory.on('updateSlot', (slot, oldItem, newItem) => {...})` provides real-time slot-level inventory updates.

## Source
Council review DEF-PAPER-4 (Confidence: 90%). The research paper says to "prefer pickup events" but never names the actual Mineflayer hook: `bot.inventory.on('updateSlot')` which is the real-time slot-level event confirmed in the Mineflayer API docs.

## Requirements
1. Add `bot.inventory.on('updateSlot')` listener in `MineflayerAdapter/index.js`
2. Emit a new `inventorySlotUpdate` event from the adapter with: slot index, old item (name+count), new item (name+count)
3. Define C# `WorldEvent` subtype: `InventorySlotUpdateEvent`
4. Add parsing in `WebSocketBridge.cs`
5. Add handler in `WorldStateProjector.cs` — `ApplyInventorySlotUpdate` should update the specific slot in `WorldState.Inventory`
6. Keep existing `BlockMinedEvent` → inventory path as a fallback/projection, but prefer `InventorySlotUpdateEvent` as the authoritative source
7. Verify this does not conflict with creative-mode `/give` inventory path

## References
- Mineflayer API: `bot.inventory.on('updateSlot')`
- `Agent.Core/WorldStateProjector.cs` — `ApplyBlockMined` inventory path
- `Agent.Core/Models/WorldState.cs` — `Inventory` dictionary
- Council review: DEF-PAPER-4
